name: Golf 365
about: Golf 365 operates a two-location golf simulation facility in Sheboygan County, designed to offer golf experiences year-round, especially during Wisconsin's harsh winters. They feature Uneekor Eye XO launch monitors and GSPro software across multiple bays. The venues combine golf technology with a full-service bar and food menu, alongside amenities like dart boards and a large projection screen, positioning themselves as a social and practical golf destination. Pricing is structured on an hourly basis per bay, with rates varying by day and time of day.
why_golfers_like_it: State-of-the-art Uneekor Eye XO launch monitors for accurate shot tracking
Option to play over 2,000 virtual golf courses from around the world
Full-service bar and food menu available at both locations for a complete experience
Seasonal leagues and tournaments for golfers of all skill levels
One bay doubles as a private studio for lessons and club fitting

food_and_drink.notes: Full kitchen and food menu; featuring brisket smashburgers at Northtown and Deo's pizzas at Indiana Avenue. Also offers fresh pizza from local restaurants.
Full service bar available. No mention of BYOB policy, implies on-site alcohol is the primary offering.

simulator_hardware.notes: Premium launch monitor used in all standard bays. One extra bay also uses this model for fitting and lessons.

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: No, club rentals are available at both locations. They also offer services like regripping, club fitting, and club sales.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: The venue has a full kitchen and bar, serving food and drinks. There is no mention of a BYOB policy, so bringing outside food or drinks is not recommended.

faq.question: How long does it typically take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: A full round of 18 holes typically takes one hour in a simulator bay.

faq.question: Is the facility kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the facility is considered kid-friendly, offering entertainment like dart boards, cornhole boards, and virtual mini-golf games in addition to the main golf simulators.
